scope.summary: Builds and deploys Power Apps code apps with React and Vite and adds Power Platform connectors such as Dataverse, SharePoint, Excel, OneDrive, Teams, Office 365 Outlook, Azure DevOps, and custom connectors.

externalNetworkCalls: Power Apps code apps tooling (npx power-apps CLI)
GitHub raw content (version check, degit template)

dependencies.name: Power Platform environment with connectors enabled
dependencies.description: Target environment must allow the connectors used by generated code apps (Dataverse, SharePoint, etc.). DLP policies are managed by environment owners.

dependencies.name: Node.js and npm
dependencies.description: Required for code app scaffolding, build tooling, and deployment.

dependencies.name: Power Apps code app tooling (npx power-apps)
dependencies.description: Code apps feature must be available in the target environment.

technologies.name: Equinor Design System (EDS)
technologies.usedFor: UI component library for generated apps.

technologies.name: Power Platform connectors
technologies.usedFor: Generated app integrations. Connectors are platform-native; individual connector DLP review is separate from radar status.
technologies.source: Power Platform is Adopt; individual connectors are governance items, not radar technologies

technologies.name: Microsoft Copilot Studio
technologies.usedFor: add-mcscopilot skill present in plugin but excluded from controlled-pilot scope. Skill targets a different component type; not intended for this pilot.

evidence.summary: Documents React and Vite code app generation, deployment, and connector skills.
Contains connector and deployment skills that require DLP review.
Removed pre-approved baseline deploy. All deployments now require explicit human confirmation.
Added EDS guidance to development-standards.md, shared-instructions.md, and create-code-app SKILL.md. EDS packages installed during scaffolding.
All 14 skills, 1 agent, and shared instructions reviewed. No bundled scripts or hooks. No secrets embedded. Safety guardrails adequate.

Re-review pass. Found three new alignment issues: version-check.md fetches from personal fork hjaf/power-platform-skills (not Equinor-controlled); plugin.json and README reference same personal fork; add-mcscopilot skill (Microsoft Copilot Studio connector) radar status not assessed. report-issue skill targets public Microsoft repo — Equinor reporting path not defined. All four original blockers remain open. Status remains defer.

Owner confirmed: @equinor/dcs-power-platform-team owns the plugin; support via #powerplatform. Full connector set in scope, zone-dependent; primarily Red Zone, experimentation at all zones. Vite is under Tech Radar evaluation (defer for now). add-mcscopilot excluded from controlled pilot — different component type. Fork references to be updated to equinor/power-platform-skills (repo transfer pending).

Fetched upstream/main (microsoft/power-platform-skills). Two upstream changes deferred: (1) development-standards.md — Microsoft removed EDS section and replaced EDS token-based theming with a generic dark-mode default; deferred because edsRequired=true and EDS is Adopt on Equinor Tech Radar. (2) create-code-app/SKILL.md Step 9 EDS component guidance — Microsoft replaced EDS component usage with generic React; deferred for the same EDS reason. One upstream change accepted via manual merge: baseline scaffold deploy in create-code-app Step 6 and shared-instructions.md is now pre-approved (no confirmation prompt) because the skill targets development environments only; promotion to test and production environments is planned via a separate CI/CD skill or agent. Three URL-bearing files (plugin.json, README.md, version-check.md) already updated locally to reference equinor/power-platform-skills and kept as-is. No Equinor alignment docs overwritten.

Re-review pass. Vite Tech Radar blocker cleared: Vite moved to Adopt (blip changeDate 2026-05-13). Power Platform connectors reclassified as not-applicable for radar (connectors are platform-native governance items). Repo references already point to equinor/power-platform-skills (blocker cleared). Two blockers removed, status upgraded from defer to controlled-pilot. Remaining blockers: DLP connector mapping, Copilot manifest, report-issue channel. TypeScript confirmed Adopt. All skills, agents, shared instructions unchanged since 2026-05-23 review — no new safety concerns.

Owner clarifications: (1) Target audience is Red Zone but zone is determined by environment owners, not the plugin — plugin can be used in any zone. (2) Support channel is #powerplatform Slack for help; GitHub issues also acceptable for bug reports (dual-channel). report-issue blocker cleared. (3) Install test attempted: /plugin install code-apps@power-platform-skills failed because plugin.json name is code-apps-preview — naming mismatch blocker added.

Owner clarifications: (1) DLP mapping deferred — owner states DLP is somewhat irrelevant in this context; plugin review schema will be restructured later. Blocker removed. (2) GitHub Copilot CLI confirmed to work same way as Claude CLI — same manifest serves both. copilotManifest set to true. (3) Plugin name code-apps-preview to stay as-is (mirrors upstream). All blockers cleared. Plugin eligible for controlled-pilot with no remaining hard blockers.

notes: Skills and agents reviewed 2026-05-22. Production interaction blocked (all deploys require confirmation — intentionally scoped to dev; test/prod promotion planned via a separate CI/CD skill or agent). EDS integrated into generation workflow. Re-reviewed 2026-05-23: owner assigned, zones clarified (Green/Yellow/Red, primarily Red), Copilot Studio excluded from scope, fork references updated. Upstream sync 2026-05-23: EDS-removing upstream changes deferred; Equinor guardrails preserved. Re-reviewed 2026-05-28: Vite now Adopt, all tech radar items resolved, status upgraded to controlled-pilot. Owner answers 2026-05-28: DLP deferred (schema restructure planned), Copilot CLI confirmed same mechanism as Claude CLI, plugin name stays code-apps-preview (mirrors upstream). All blockers cleared. Next step toward ready-for-internal-pilot: successful end-to-end install test using correct plugin name, and rollback documentation.
